Croton eriocarpum, also known as the Croton tree, is a plant species native to tropical Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its leaves have been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Croton eriocarpum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Croton eriocarpum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
